title: Radiology Reading Turnaround Time
app: RIS/PACS integration via HL7 or HEC
spl:
index=healthcare sourcetype="ris:report"
| eval tat_min=round((report_finalized_time-exam_complete_time)/60,1)
| where tat_min > 0
| stats avg(tat_min) as avg_tat, perc95(tat_min) as p95_tat by modality, priority
| sort priority, -p95_tat
| table modality, priority, avg_tat, p95_tat
description: Radiology report TAT affects clinical decisions and patient discharge timing. Tracking by modality and priority ensures SLA compliance and identifies workflow bottlenecks.
value: Service and risk leaders use signals from Radiology Reading Turnaround Time to move crews, budget, and customer or regulator communications while the situation is still controllable, so the organization steers from live data rather than a post-incident report.
implementation: Parse RIS events for exam completion and report finalization timestamps. Track by modality (CT, MR, XR) and priority (stat, urgent, routine). Alert on stat studies exceeding 1-hour TAT. **Domain context:** Joint Commission and operational targets often use modality-specific TAT; peer review and resident reads can skew distributionsŌĆösegment when possible. **Splunk:** Use `strptime` if times are strings; ensure `exam_complete_time` reflects acquisition end, not order start.
visualization: Bar chart (TAT by modality), Line chart (TAT trend), Table (exams exceeding SLA).
knownFalsePositives: Reading backlog spikes when the worklist floods after PACS upgrades, telerad overflow, addendum policies, or peer-learning conferences thin staffing.

references.title: Splunk Lantern ŌĆö use case library

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
ŌĆó Install and configure the required add-on or app: RIS/PACS integration via HL7 or HEC.
ŌĆó Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=healthcare` `sourcetype="ris:report"` fields `accession`, `modality`, `priority`, `exam_complete_time`, `report_finalized_time`.
ŌĆó For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ŌĆö Configure data collection
Parse RIS events for exam completion and report finalization timestamps. Track by modality (CT, MR, XR) and priority (stat, urgent, routine). Alert on stat studies exceeding 1-hour TAT. **Domain context:** Joint Commission and operational targets often use modality-specific TAT; peer review and resident reads can skew distributionsŌĆösegment when possible. **Splunk:** Use `strptime` if times are strings; ensure `exam_complete_time` reflects acquisition end, not order start.

Step 2 ŌĆö Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=healthcare sourcetype="ris:report"
| eval tat_min=round((report_finalized_time-exam_complete_time)/60,1)
| where tat_min > 0
| stats avg(tat_min) as avg_tat, perc95(tat_min) as p95_tat by modality, priority
| sort priority, -p95_tat
| table modality, priority, avg_tat, p95_tat
```

Understanding this SPL

**Radiology Reading Turnaround Time** ŌĆö Radiology report TAT affects clinical decisions and patient discharge timing. Tracking by modality and priority ensures SLA compliance and identifies workflow bottlenecks.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=healthcare` `sourcetype="ris:report"` fields `accession`, `modality`, `priority`, `exam_complete_time`, `report_finalized_time`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): RIS/PACS integration via HL7 or HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedŌĆörename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: healthcare; **sourcetype**: ris:report. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

ŌĆó Scopes the data: index=healthcare, sourcetype="ris:report". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
ŌĆó `eval` defines or adjusts **tat_min** ŌĆö often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
ŌĆó Filters the current rows with `where tat_min > 0` ŌĆö typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
ŌĆó `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by modality, priority** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
ŌĆó Orders rows with `sort` ŌĆö combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
ŌĆó Pipeline stage (see **Radiology Reading Turnaround Time**): table modality, priority, avg_tat, p95_tat


Step 3 ŌĆö Validate
Compare Splunk results with the EHR, LIS, pharmacy, or device vendor view and spot-check fields against the Epic/Cerner (or equivalent) audit export for the same user, patient context, or device and time range.

Step 4 ŌĆö Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (TAT by modality), Line chart (TAT trend), Table (exams exceeding SLA).
grandmaExplanation: We watch how quickly imaging gets read. We help you catch backlog before cancer and ED pathways stall.
